title: UBetterOS Marketplace

description.paragraph1: A company developed a modular operating system for neural implants.
description.paragraph2: The system core is extremely small: it only manages brain memory, authentication, and basic communication.
description.paragraph3: Everything else works through plug‑ins: night vision, mental ad filtering, instant translation, cringe‑thought blocker, and even courage simulators for meetings.
description.paragraph4: The problem started when users installed hundreds of plug‑ins created by AI without any quality standard.
description.paragraph5: Now the system continues to work… but some people hear energy drink ads every time they blink.

bullet_point.bullet1: New functionalities must be added [b]without changing the core[/b].
bullet_point.bullet2: Failures in one plug‑in must not bring down the entire system.

footnote: [i]~ Where is the premium option to get rid of the ad?[/i]

bug1.description: A 'personal motivation' plug‑in started crashing constantly after an update. Fortunately, the rest of the operating system continued functioning normally. Which characteristic of Microkernel architecture explains this behavior?
